channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Lula diz que governo pretende capturar os ‘bandidos do andar de cima’
item.description: President Lula stated that his government aims to capture the "high-level criminals" of organized crime, criticing operations that focus only on favelas and the poor. He emphasized the need to arrest criminals living in high-end condominiums and mentioned cooperation with the U.S. to extradite Brazilians involved in crime who reside in Miami. Lula also referenced Donald Trump's request to contribute to the extradition of Brazilian citizens linked to drug trafficking.

item.title: Após Flávio chamar Lula de 'Opala velhão', petista rebate e diz que Bolsonaro 'está no desmanche'
item.description: President Lula retaliated against Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's 'old Opala' comment by saying he once owned a turbocharged 1994 Opala and that Bolsonaro's 'Opala' — referring to Jair Bolsonaro — is 'in the scrapyard'. He made the remarks during the launch of the Federative Caravan in Niterói, where he also promoted the Anti-Criminal Organization Law and urged men to overcome fear of prostate exams.

item.title: Governo Lula assina decreto que reestrutura Coaf com objetivo de fortalecer combate ao crime organizado
item.description: The Lula government issued a decree to restructure the Coaf (Financial Activities Control Council), aiming to strengthen the fight against organized crime and financial crimes. The measure seeks to expand the agency’s reach and effectiveness following incidents that exposed weaknesses in the financial monitoring system. The reform reflects the government’s effort to enhance transparency and security in combating money laundering and terrorist financing.

item.title: “Intocáveis de sempre contra os cidadãos de bem”, diz Zema sobre STF
item.description: Former Minas Gerais governor Romeu Zema (Novo) criticized the STF for overturning a decision that gave 48 hours to investigate alleged theft from retirees, claiming the 'untouchables' of the Court ordered the investigation to stop. He said this represents an attack on 'decent citizens' and reinforced his narrative of fighting impunity. The video was posted on X (formerly Twitter).

item.title: Lula diz não se ofender por ser chamado de Opala por Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) responded with humor to Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's (PL-RJ) comment calling him an 'Opala', stating that Jair Bolsonaro 'is in the junkyard'. Lula said he was not offended by the nickname and used it to mock the former president's political situation. The remarks were made during a public event on Thursday, the 26th.

item.title: Lula rebate Flávio por comparação com Opala: ‘é o pai dele, que está no desmanche’
item.description: President Lula rebutted Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's comparison of him to an 'old Opala', saying he is not offended by the analogy but that Flávio's father, Jair Bolsonaro, is the one 'in the junkyard'. Lula noted he once owned a turbocharged 1994 Opala and criticized the Bolsonaro family's management of federal hospitals in Rio, declaring this the 'year of truth' in the battle between truth and lies.

item.title: Papo Antagonista: Antagonista entrevista pré-candidatos à Presidência: Romeu Zema
item.description: Minas Gerais Governor Romeu Zema was interviewed on Papo Antagonista about his projects for Brazil's future, highlighting his views as a potential presidential candidate. The segment also covered the activities of councilman Adrilles Jorge, including the incident where he wore a wig and lipstick during an Alesp session.

item.title: “Pai dele que está em desmanche”, diz Lula a Flávio
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva countered Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's remark calling him an 'old Opala', stating instead that former President Jair Bolsonaro is the one 'in junkyard'. The exchange of insults took place during an event in Rio de Janeiro on Thursday, March 26. Lula turned the old car metaphor against Flávio's father, reversing the accusation.

item.title: Lula destaca relação de parceria entre governo federal e prefeituras
item.description: President Lula emphasized the importance of partnership between the federal government and municipal authorities to solve local problems during the opening of the 18th Federative Caravan in Rio de Janeiro. He stressed that national success depends on well-functioning cities and announced deliveries of dental units, equipment kits, and ambulances for municipalities in the state. The mayor of Niterói praised the federal government’s democratic and collaborative approach, contrasting it with authoritarian trends in Latin America.

item.title: Por 8 votos a 2, Supremo derruba prorrogação da CPMI do INSS
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court overturned, by a vote of 8 to 2, the extension of the INSS CPMI ordered by Minister André Mendonça, setting the conclusion of the commission's work for Saturday (28). The commission's president, Senator Carlos Viana (Podemos-MG), expects the final report to be read and voted on Friday (27). The CPMI was established in August 2025 to investigate undue deductions from retirees' and pensioners' INSS benefits.

item.title: “CNN Brasil” nomeia João Vitor Xavier como novo CEO
item.description: CNN Brasil appointed journalist and radio host João Vitor Xavier as its new CEO, announced on Thursday, March 26, 2026. Xavier, who also leads Rádio Itatiaia — the country’s top-rated radio station — will hold both positions following an invitation from majority shareholder Rubens Menin. He confirmed he will not run for re-election in 2026 to focus solely on managing the two media outlets, emphasizing his commitment to democracy and public information in Brazil.

item.title: Versão ‘moderada’ de Flávio já encomenda primeira crise com ala radical do bolsonarismo
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro, the PL presidential candidate, faced backlash from the radical wing of Bolsonarismo for supporting a bill that equates misogyny with racism, which conservatives view as a left-leaning proposal. Despite internal opposition, his moderate stance may be a strategic move to appeal to conservative voters not aligned with radical Bolsonarismo. He currently ties with Lula in voter intention polls, surprising even his most optimistic supporters.

item.title: Cortejado pelo PSB, Pacheco só definirá filiação no limite da janela
item.description: Senator Rodrigo Pacheco (PSD-MG) will wait until the party-switching window closes on April 3 to decide his new party, aiming to run for governor of Minas Gerais. He had dinner with PSB national president João Campos in Brasília, signaling interest in the party, but is still awaiting potential offers from MDB and União Brasil, which could provide more TV time and municipal infrastructure. Pacheco must leave the PSD because current vice-governor Mateus Simões, also from PSD and allied with Governor Romeu Zema, blocks his candidacy under that party.

item.title: Os sete recados do STF ao Congresso e ao próprio tribunal no julgamento que encerrou a CPI do INSS
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court (STF) rejected the extension of the INSS CPI, setting clear limits on parliamentary inquiries and reinforcing the separation of powers by stating that the duration of CPIs is the responsibility of Congress. Ministers also indicated that INSS frauds should be investigated by the Judiciary to avoid political exploitation in an election year, and protected Justices Alexandre de Moraes and Dias Toffoli from inquiries related to the Banco Master scandal.

item.title: Pesquisa expõe mudança no perfil histórico de apoio a Lula e traz sinal de alerta na campanha
item.description: A recent AtlasIntel poll shows 72% of Brazilians aged 16 to 24 disapprove of President Lula, marking a historic reversal in his electoral base, which once relied strongly on youth support. Approval is now concentrated among voters aged 45 to 59, highlighting a structural shift in Lula’s coalition. Analysts argue that the right’s superior digital communication strategy on social media has enabled young people to become opinion multipliers, amplifying their political influence beyond direct voting.

item.title: Entenda a decisão e os próximos passos da extradição de Carla Zambelli
item.description: On Thursday (26), the Italian Justice authorized the extradition of former deputy Carla Zambelli (PL) so she can serve a prison sentence in Brazil. The decision responds to a request from the Brazilian Judiciary, seeking to enforce a final conviction already ruled against the lawmaker.

item.title: Lula fala em medo de “dedada” e cobra exame de próstata de homens
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva urged men to undergo prostate exams, criticizing the fear of the 'digital rectal exam' and emphasizing cancer prevention. He said he plans to accompany First Lady Janja Lula da Silva in her routine check-ups to set an example. The remarks were made during the Caravana Federativa in Niterói, where the government also announced health investments, including ambulances, dental units, and funding for specialist programs.

item.title: ‘Funk do 01’ usado por Flávio não é propaganda eleitoral antecipada, diz advogado
item.description: Lawyer Vitor Barretta states that the use of the funk 'Zero Um, Novo Capitão' by Flávio Bolsonaro does not constitute early electoral propaganda, as there is no explicit or implicit request for votes. The song’s use in his pre-campaign is legal and aims to broaden his appeal beyond the Bolsonarist base.

item.title: Lula diz não se ofender por ser chamado de Opala por Flávio e fala que Bolsonaro ‘está no desmanche’
item.description: President Lula responded to Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's comparison of him to an 'old Opala', saying he is not offended and that Bolsonaro 'is in the junkyard', referring to his imprisonment and hospitalization. Lula also defended his health and fitness at 80, criticized the Bolsonaro family's management of federal hospitals in Rio de Janeiro, and attacked former governor Cláudio Castro.

item.title: STF forma maioria para derrubar liminar de Mendonça pela prorrogação da CPI mista do INSS
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court (STF) formed a majority to overturn Minister André Mendonça's injunction that had extended the mixed INSS CPI's work by 120 days, halting investigations into billion-dollar fraud schemes involving deductions from retirees' and pensioners' benefits. The ruling was supported by ministers Flávio Dino, Alexandre de Moraes, Nunes Marques, Cristiano Zanin, Dias Toffoli and Cármen Lúcia, opposed only by Mendonça and Luiz Fux. The CPI had been probing potential links involving Fábio Luís Lula da Silva and banker Daniel Vorcaro, who is in preventive detention and negotiating a plea deal.

item.title: Gilmar Mendes diz que vazamento de mensagens de Vorcaro foi “criminoso”
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Gilmar Mendes labeled the leak of Daniel Vorcaro's messages as criminal and abominable, stating that unjustified breach of secrecy is unconstitutional and illegal. He made the remarks during the ruling that blocked the extension of the INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee, addressing senators Carlos Viana and Alfredo Gaspar, who were present in the plenary. Justice Alexandre de Moraes agreed, while Minister André Mendonça asserted that decisions were properly executed and emphasized the need to investigate the leak, which hinders the investigation into Vorcaro's alleged misconduct.

item.title: Lula cita gastos com cães e diz que chineses não têm esse problema
item.description: President Lula stated that pet-related expenses in Brazil have risen over the decades and now constitute a 'seizure of salary,' citing costs such as pet dentistry, weekly baths, and veterinary care. He contrasted this with the alleged absence of such expenses among Chinese people, a remark that may be seen as prejudiced due to its reliance on a stereotype. Lula also urged Finance Minister Dario Durigan to address Brazilian household debt, proposing financial literacy campaigns on television.

item.title: Eleição na Alerj cria mais um nó jurídico e sucessão no Rio depende de tribunal
item.description: The election for the presidency of the Rio de Janeiro State Legislature (Alerj) has sparked judicial challenges from the opposition, who argue the process was illegal due to insufficient notice and lack of genuine debate. Douglas Ruas, a pre-candidate for governor in 2026, was elected with 45 of 70 votes and may assume interim control of the Guanabara Palace if the ruling stands. The PL is positioning Ruas as a candidate in the indirect elections to complete Cláudio Castro’s term, pending a Supreme Federal Court (STF) decision on the electoral rules.

item.title: Sem prorrogação, CPI corre para terminar trabalhos até sábado
item.description: The INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPMI) must conclude its work by Saturday (March 28, 2026) after the Supreme Federal Court rejected the extension requested by Minister André Mendonça. The final report, drafted by rapporteur Alfredo Gaspar (União Brasil-AL), exceeds 5,000 pages and recommends indicting 228 individuals, though President Lula’s son, Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, will not be indicted due to lack of evidence, only mentioned. The government coalition is preparing a parallel report and may request a viewing delay to postpone the vote.

item.title: Edison Garcia deixa CEB IPEs, mas permanece à frente da holding
item.description: Edison Garcia will leave his position as president of CEB IPEs but will remain in charge of the CEB holding company. His departure coincides with the end of Governor Ibaneis Rocha's term, who is resigning to run for Senate. During his tenure, Garcia highlighted the full replacement of public lighting with LED technology in the Federal District.

item.title: Supremo barra CPMI do INSS e manda Congresso encerrar investigação
item.description: The Brazilian Supreme Federal Court overturned Justice André Mendonça's injunction that had ordered the Senate to extend the INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPMI), ruling that only the President of the National Congress, Senator Davi Alcolumbre, has the authority to extend the committee’s mandate. The decision passed by an 8–2 vote, with Justices Dino, Zanin, Moraes, Marques, Cármen Lúcia, Toffoli, Mendes, and Fachin in favor, while Mendonça and Fux dissented. The Court held that the refusal to act on the CPMI’s request falls within the discretionary powers of the Congressional president, effectively ending the investigation into fraudulent consigned loans to INSS retirees and pensioners.

item.title: Eleitor em dia: com qual idade posso tirar o título?
item.description: In Brazil, individuals can apply for a voter ID at age 15, but must turn 16 by election day to be eligible to vote. For the 2026 General Elections, the deadline is October 4, the date of the first round. Voting is mandatory from age 18 and optional for illiterate citizens and those over 70.

item.title: Com eleição na Alerj, Rio tem três governadores em apenas quatro dias
item.description: The resignation of Governor Cláudio Castro (PL) following a TSE investigation into 2022 election fraud, combined with the removal of Alerj president-elect Rodrigo Bacellar (União) over suspicions of leaking operations to organized crime, resulted in Rio de Janeiro having three governors in four days. Douglas Ruas (PL), the newly elected Alerj president, assumed interim control of the state until indirect elections in April, when deputies will vote to choose the next governor.

item.title: A gafe do presidente Lula envolvendo chineses e cachorros
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva made a gaffe by joking about stereotypes regarding dog meat consumption in China during an event at the CAOA factory in Anápolis (GO), in front of Chinese businessmen. The comment, while drawing laughter from the audience, was criticized for reinforcing an outdated prejudice, as the Chinese government banned the consumption of pet meat years ago. Lula also discussed government proposals, including social media regulation and income tax exemption for those earning up to R$ 5,000, amid a tightening electoral race for 2026.

item.title: Nova lei estabelece condições mínimas de infraestrutura para escolas públicas
item.description: Law 15.360, signed by President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, establishes minimum infrastructure standards for public basic education schools, including adequate student-teacher ratios, libraries, science and computer labs, internet access, covered sports courts, kitchens, cafeterias, bathrooms, accessibility, electricity, treated water, sanitation, and solid waste management. The law amends the National Education Guidelines and Framework Law (LDB) and originated from Senate Bill 5.288/2019 by Senator Flávio Arns (PSB-PR), approved in April 2024 under the rapporteurship of Senator Professora Dorinha Seabra (União-TO).

item.title: Tarcísio diz que está ‘acostumado às bobagens’ de Lula e que governo federal ‘vive de narrativas’
item.description: São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas dismissed President Lula's criticisms as 'nonsense,' saying he is accustomed to them and that the federal government lives on narratives and propaganda without delivering real results for state projects. He downplayed federal involvement in works such as the Rodoanel and the Intercity Train, claiming no modeling, design, or execution by the Union. Tarcísio also suggested the federal government is worried about electoral defeat, citing polls showing Flávio Bolsonaro ahead of Lula in a potential runoff.

item.title: LDO: vai à sanção ajuste para nova licença-paternidade e apoio à reciclagem
item.description: PLP 77/2026, authored by Senator Randolfe Rodrigues (PT-AP), has been approved by Congress and awaits presidential sanction. The bill amends the 2026 Budget Guidelines Law (LDO) to exclude INSS-funded paternity leave expenses from the fiscal arcabouço, as the benefit increases from 5 to 20 days between 2027 and 2029, and allows PIS/Cofins tax credits for recycling and free-trade zone companies — a benefit set to expire in 2027 with the tax reform that replaces PIS/Cofins with the CBS.

item.title: Tarcísio diz que governo Lula ‘vive de narrativas’ e que ‘perdeu a conexão com as pessoas’
item.description: São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas criticized the Lula government for 'living on narratives' and having 'lost connection with the people', claiming federal actions are out of touch with public reality. He announced R$159 million in funding for 12,500 new Real Estate Credit Letters (CCI) under the Casa Paulista program, defending the state’s housing policy against accusations of plagiarizing federal initiatives and downplaying federal involvement. Tarcísio also questioned barriers to resettling families from inadequate conditions, such as in the Moinho favela, arguing that tangible results foster stronger public connection than empty propaganda or narratives.

item.title: Ao vivo: Lula abre a Caravana Federativa do Rio
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva inaugurated the Federative Caravan of Rio in Niterói, aiming to strengthen dialogue between the federal government and states/municipalities, expanding access to public programs and investments. The event includes announcements for the State of Rio de Janeiro, workshops, service booths, and dialogues with state and municipal officials.

item.title: Douglas Ruas é eleito presidente da Alerj e será governador do Rio
item.description: Douglas Ruas was elected president of the Alerj with 45 votes in favor, assuming interim governorship of Rio de Janeiro following the TSE's cassation of Cláudio Castro and Rodrigo Bacellar. The vote proceeded amid an opposition boycott and the absence of 22 deputies. The TSE ruling also declared Castro and Bacellar ineligible until 2030 for abuse of political and economic power.

item.title: PF suspeita que Lulinha cogitou fugir do país
item.description: The Federal Police suspect that Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, known as Lulinha, considered fleeing Brazil after calling President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva to discuss his continued stay in Spain, where he has resided with his family since mid-2024. The contact occurred after the PF forwarded a report to Supreme Court Minister André Mendonça regarding possible irregularities involving Lulinha. The investigation examines whether there is an attempt to evade justice.

item.title: Governo garante abastecimento de diesel em março e abril
item.description: The Ministry of Mines and Energy confirmed normal diesel supply for March and April 2026, with supply exceeding demand, following analysis by the Fuel Supply Monitoring Room. The government also fined 16 fuel distributors for abusive pricing practices during inspections from March 9 to 25, with some margins increasing up to 270% weekly, involving joint task forces from MJSP, MME, Senacon, PF, and ANP.

item.title: Inelegível, Castro é convidado por Ciro Nogueira para o PP
item.description: Senator Ciro Nogueira, president of the Progressistas party, invited former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro (PL) to join the party, despite Castro’s 8-year ineligibility imposed by the TSE for abuse of power in the 2022 elections. Nogueira believes the decision will be reversed and sees Castro as a strong potential Senate candidate in 2026, praising his integrity and poll leadership. Castro resigned one day before the judgment and stated he leaves with his head high, trusting justice will restore his electoral rights.

item.title: Ao vivo: STF julga se mantém prorrogação da CPMI do INSS
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court is reviewing in a virtual plenary whether to uphold Minister André Mendonça’s decision to extend the INSS CPMI by 60 days, which investigates fraud in social security benefits. Mendonça ruled that the extension is a prerogative of the parliamentary minority and that the Senate president cannot refuse to analyze the request. The ruling is being considered as a referendum on the injunction granted on March 23, 2026.

item.title: Para Lula, culpa do endividamento brasileiro é dos “cachorrinhos”
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva claimed that Brazilian household debt stems from excessive spending on pets, referring to them as 'little dogs,' and suggested China does not face this issue. The remark was made during an event with Chinese representatives at an automobile factory in Anápolis (GO) and drew laughter from the audience.

item.title: Aliado de Flávio Bolsonaro, Douglas Ruas é eleito presidente da Alerj e assumirá governo do Rio
item.description: Deputy Douglas Ruas (PL), an ally of Flávio Bolsonaro, was elected president of Alerj with 45 out of 46 votes from present legislators. He was nominated by Flávio Bolsonaro as the Rio de Janeiro gubernatorial candidate for the 2026 elections. The election took place amid criticism from the opposition.

item.title: Ex-BBB Matteus Amaral será candidato a deputado federal; entenda
item.description: Former Big Brother Brasil 2024 contestant Matteus Amaral, the season's runner-up, has joined the Progressistas (PP) party and plans to run for a federal deputy seat in the 2026 elections. His move into politics represents the first attempt by a former BBB participant to transition into a federal legislative role in this electoral cycle.

item.title: Depois de 4 meses, Lula vai enviar indicação de Messias ao Senado
item.description: President Lula is set to send on Friday (March 27, 2026) the nomination of AGU Minister Jorge Messias to the STF, to fill the vacancy left by Roberto Barroso four months ago. Despite having only 25 declared senatorial supports — below the 41 needed — the government claims the political climate has improved and that the nomination is a presidential prerogative not to be indefinitely delayed. The process now depends on Senate President Davi Alcolumbre scheduling the hearing in the CCJ, where Messias currently has 10 of the 14 required votes.

item.title: ‘Quem não tem o que mostrar, vive de narrativa’, diz Tarcísio sobre Lula
item.description: São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas criticized President Lula for celebrating federal credit releases from BNDES for projects like the intercity train and Metro Line-2, stating these are federal obligations, not favors. He remarked, 'Those who have nothing to say live on narrative,' implying the federal government relies on empty rhetoric to promote itself.

item.title: O entrosamento de Eduardo Leite e Kassab em evento em São Paulo
item.description: Governor Eduardo Leite and PSD president Gilberto Kassab attended the launch of the book 'Juntos chegaremos lá!' by Guilherme Afif Domingos in São Paulo. Leite reiterated his focus on securing his presidential candidacy within the party, stating he would only leave the governorship for a larger project, while Kassab called Ratinho Junior’s withdrawal from the race 'surprising'. The event highlighted the growing alignment between PSDB and PSD leaders ahead of the 2026 elections.

item.title: Lula diz que brasileiros gastam muito com cachorros e que a China 'não deve ter esse problema'
item.description: President Lula stated that Brazilians spend excessively on pet care, contrasting it with China, where he claims this issue does not exist. The remark sparked debate over household spending priorities and animal welfare in Brazil's economic context.

item.title: Eduardo Leite afirma que Caiado é candidato de grupo que 'já tem representante' e se diz animado com possível candidatura à Presidência
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite stated that Ronaldo Caiado already has representation within the PSD and expressed enthusiasm about potentially becoming the party’s presidential candidate in 2026. He emphasized the need for a centrist candidacy that is tough on crime yet sensitive to social needs, reducing public costs without offending anyone. Leite ruled out a Senate run and said the PSD’s final decision will be made on Tuesday (31) after internal consultations.

item.title: O filho pródigo
item.description: Sergio Moro reconciled with Bolsonarism after a 2020 rupture, joined the PL party, and launched his candidacy for governor of Paraná alongside Flávio Bolsonaro, Deltan Dallagnol, and Filipe Barros, forming a 'pure-blood' ticket. He criticized the União Brasil for failing to support him and stated that politics is made with people of one's word.

item.title: Sem acordo
item.description: Governor Carlos Brandão (unaffiliated) and Vice Governor Felipe Camarão (PT) are in political and judicial rupture in Maranhão, with Brandão appointing his nephew Daniel Brandão to the TCE and Camarão facing money laundering allegations. The progressive coalition that supported Flávio Dino is collapsing, threatening the PT's gubernatorial candidacy and dividing Lula's electoral slate in the state, while Dino retains influence through allies.

item.title: Fora do baralho
item.description: Former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro was sentenced by the TSE to eight years of political inelegibility for abuse of political and economic power in the clandestine hiring of 27,000 electoral workers in 2022, despite his resignation. The ruling also stripped Rodrigo Bacellar, former president of the state legislature, of his mandate and eligibility, complicating the PL’s Senate slate plans in Rio, as both are now barred from office until 2030.
